description_wiki: Vielsalm (French pronunciation: [vjɛlsalm]; Walloon: Li Viye Såm) is a municipality of Wallonia located in the province of Luxembourg, Belgium.
Belgium, officially the Kingdom of Belgium, is a country in Northwestern Europe.
The 82nd Airborne Division ("All American")  is a United States Army airborne infantry division that specializes in parachute assault, based in Fort Bragg, North Carolina.

description: unified armed forces of Nazi Germany from 1935 to 1945
description_wiki: The Wehrmacht (German pronunciation: [ˈveːɐ̯maxt] , lit. 'defence force') were the unified armed forces of Nazi Germany from 1935 to 1945.
Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: dictator of Germany from 1933 to 1945, main instigator of World War II (1889–1945)
description_wiki: Adolf Hitler (20 April 1889 – 30 April 1945) was an Austrian-born  German politician who was the dictator of Germany during the Nazi era from 1933 until his suicide in 1945.

description: most populous city in North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany
description_wiki: Cologne (  kə-LOHN; German: Köln [kœln] ; Kölsch: Kölle [ˈkœ̂lə] ) is the fourth-most populous city of Germany and the largest city of the German state of North Rhine-Westphalia with nearly 1.1 million inhabitants in the city proper and over 3.1 million people in the Cologne Bonn urban region.

description: city in the federal state of Thuringia, Germany
description_wiki: Weimar is a city in the German state of Thuringia, in Central Germany between Erfurt to the west and Jena to the east, 80 km (50 mi) southwest of Leipzig, 170 km (106 mi) north of Nuremberg and 170 km (106 mi) west of Dresden.

surface_forms: a nearby concentration camp named Buchenwald
description_wiki: Buchenwald (German pronunciation: [ˈbuːxn̩valt]; 'beech forest') was a German Nazi concentration camp established on Ettersberg hill near Weimar, Germany, in July 1937.
The V-1 flying bomb (German: Vergeltungswaffe 1, lit. 'Vengeance Weapon 1') was an early cruise missile.

aliases: Allies of the Second World War
description: grouping of the victorious countries of World War II
description_wiki: The Allies, or Allied powers, formally referred to as the United Nations from 1942, were an international military coalition formed during World War II (1939–1945) to oppose the Axis powers.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: England is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

Ernest Taylor Pyle (August 3, 1900 – April 18, 1945) was an American journalist and war correspondent who is best known for his stories about ordinary American soldiers during World War II. Pyle is also notable for the columns he wrote as a roving human-interest reporter from 1935 through 1941 for the Scripps-Howard newspaper syndicate that earned him wide acclaim for his simple accounts of ordinary people across North America.

description_wiki: The Battle of the Bulge, also known as the Ardennes Offensive, was the last major German offensive campaign on the Western Front during the Second World War, taking place from 16 December 1944 to 25 January 1945.
